data.title: Cosmos Labs Engages in Talks with Circle Regarding Native USDC Issuance
data.description: Cosmos Labs is reportedly in discussions with Circle about issuing a native USDC, according to Foresight News. The information was shared by tøny on Twitter, citing a recent Telegram chat where Cosmos Labs Co-CEO magmar disclosed the ongoing talks between the two teams.

data.title: WLFI advisor ogle went long on ZRO with 5x leverage and even added to his spot position two weeks ago.
data.description: Lookonchain monitoring shows that WLFI advisor ogle (@cryptogle) went long 347,280 ZRO (worth $795,000) 5x in the past 2 hours. Two weeks ago, he also spent $50,000 to buy 33,411 ZRO (currently worth $755,000).

data.title: Makina: The funds held by MEV Builder have been returned and transferred to a dedicated recovery account.
data.description: Makina, the DeFi execution engine that was previously hacked, posted on its official X account that at 21:15 on January 22, MEV Builder returned the funds according to the SEAL white hat safe harbor protocol and deducted 10% of the bounty, returning approximately 920 ETH (out of a total of 1023 ETH), accounting for approximately 1299 ETH of the total stolen funds; the funds have been transferred to the recovery multisignature address 0xc22F…8AB9, and the team is continuing to pursue the remaining funds and seeking to contact the RocketPool validator address 0x573D…910E, which received approximately 276 ETH.

data.title: MilkyWay has completed the final protocol revenue distribution to the community.
data.description: MilkyWay announced the completion of its final protocol revenue distribution to the community, distributing a total of 92,708 USDC. This amount was converted from fees collected in the form of TIA, INIT, and BABY tokens. The distribution was based on a final protocol snapshot, and eligible participants included MILK holders, MILK stakers, and liquidity providers. A minimum threshold of 1 USDC was set for this distribution; addresses with less than 1 USDC were not eligible. Previously, MilkyWay announced it was gradually ceasing operations and would permanently shut down. MilkyWay stated that the demand and adoption of decentralized finance had not met expectations, and the WayCard launch was too late to alleviate funding pressures in a timely manner.

data.title: XRP Spot ETF Sees Significant Inflow on January 22
data.description:
On January 22, XRP spot ETF experienced a net inflow of $2.09 million, according to ChainCatcher. This inflow was solely attributed to the Franklin XRP ETF XRPZ, marking a single-day net inflow of $2.09 million and bringing the historical total net inflow to $293 million.

As of the latest update, the total net asset value of the XRP spot ETF stands at $1.37 billion, with an XRP net asset ratio of 1.17%. The cumulative historical net inflow has reached $1.23 billion.

data.title: Ethereum Mainnet Surpasses Layer 2 Networks in Daily Active Addresses
data.description: Ethereum's mainnet has seen a significant increase in daily active addresses, surpassing all Layer 2 networks. According to ChainCatcher, data from Token Terminal indicates that the number of active addresses on Ethereum's mainnet has exceeded those on Layer 2 solutions. This development highlights Ethereum's continued dominance in the blockchain space, as it maintains a higher level of user engagement compared to its Layer 2 counterparts.

data.title: Trump Sues JPMorgan Over Alleged Political Debanking
data.description:
Donald Trump files a $5B lawsuit against JPMorgan for alleged political debanking; no crypto link.
source: https://coincu.com/news/trump-jpmorgan-political-lawsuit/

data.title: Makina Announces Return of Funds to MEV Builders
data.description: Makina, a multi-chain DeFi execution engine, announced via Twitter that funds held by MEV builders have been returned. According to Foresight News, a 10% bounty was deducted as part of the SEAL White Hat Safe Harbor program. This means approximately 920 ETH was returned from the 1023 ETH initially received by MEV builders, while the total amount exploited was around 1299 ETH. The funds have been transferred to a dedicated recovery account. The primary focus remains on recovering the remaining funds, and any plans or timelines for distribution will be announced when feasible.

data.title: Sky has removed the SPK rewards for staking Sky, which, combined with buybacks, could reduce net SPK emissions by 40%.
data.description: Sky has removed Spark (SPK) token rewards for staking SKY tokens. Sky stated in an X post that this move will reduce SPK emissions by approximately 30%. Combined with the proposed update to Spark buyback parameters, these adjustments are expected to reduce net SPK emissions by 40%. Based on current net revenue and SPK price, this equates to a reduction of approximately $23 million in emissions over the next 12 months.

data.title: The Bank of Japan kept interest rates unchanged.
data.description: The Bank of Japan kept its benchmark interest rate unchanged at 0.75%, in line with market expectations. (Jinshi)

data.title: Token Terminal: The number of daily active addresses on the Ethereum mainnet has surpassed all Layer 2 addresses.
data.description: Data from Token Terminal shows that the number of daily active addresses on the Ethereum mainnet has surpassed that of all Layer 2 networks.

data.title: Vitalik: This year is the year of "regaining computing autonomy," and I suggest integrating technologies such as ZKP, TEE, and FHE with local deployment of LLM.
data.description: In a post today, Vitalik Buterin stated that 2026 will be the year of regaining computing self-sovereignty, emphasizing that he has begun to reduce data leaks to centralized services. He mentioned that last year he switched to Fileverse encrypted documents and Signal/Simplex/Session messaging applications (all decentralized open-source applications), and in 2026 he will further shift to OpenStreetMap/Protonmail and decentralized social networking to protect location and communication privacy. Vitalik also discussed the progress and pain points of locally deployed LLM, suggesting a future path to efficient privacy computing through a combination of technologies such as ZKP, TEE, and FHE.

data.title: Trump’s ‘Tariff Playbook’ Hits Crypto Hard
data.description:
President Trump utilized weekend illiquidity in digital markets to issue new tariff threats against eight European nations on Saturday. The timing coincided with the one-year anniversary of the administration’s inauguration.   Analysts describe the current volatility as the middle phase of a scripted “Tariff Playbook. ” The White House is currently using these threats to force negotiations related to the acquisition of Greenland
source: https://news.shib.io/2026/01/23/trumps-tariff-playbook-hits-crypto-hard/

data.title: BitGo Debuts on NYSE in Year's First Major Crypto IPO, Ondo Tokenizes Stock Hours Later
data.description:
Custody firm's stock climbs 36% intraday before settling 2. 7% up at $18. 49.
source: https://www.blockhead.co/2026/01/23/bitgo-debuts-on-nyse-in-years-first-major-crypto-ipo-ondo-tokenizes-stock-hours-later/

data.title: Robinhood is now available on ASTER and LDO.
data.description: Robinhood announced this morning that Robinhood Legend has launched trading services for ASTER and LDO.

data.title: USD.AI approves up to $500 million in GPU funding for Sharon AI.
data.description: USD.AI has officially announced that it has approved a maximum of $500 million in asset-backed, non-recourse debt financing for GPU deployments by Australian AI computing power provider Sharon AI, using stablecoin liquidity through an on-chain credit system. Sharon AI expects to launch the financing program as early as the first quarter of 2026, providing funding for an initial $65 million GPU deployment.

data.title: casualpig.eth withdrew 10 million ENA from Bybit and staked them.
data.description: Lookonchain monitoring shows that the whale casualpig.eth withdrew 10 million ENA (US$1.75 million) from Bybit 12 hours ago and staked them.

data.title: The ELSA team transferred $4 million worth of tokens to Bybit yesterday, causing the token price to fluctuate wildly in the early morning.
data.description: According to onchainschool.pro monitoring, $4 million worth of ELSA was transferred from one team wallet to two newly created wallets last night. Subsequently, these tokens were sent to Bybit. Market data also shows that ELSA experienced a rollercoaster ride, surging 173% at 5 AM this morning before plummeting 38% in the following hour. As of press time, ELSA is trading at $0.21459, with a circulating market capitalization of $48.54 million.

data.title: YZi Labs Invests in BitGo's IPO
data.description: YZi Labs announced on X its strategic investment in BitGo's IPO as it debuts on the NYSE, backing "inevitably vital" regulated infrastructure required to onboard the next trillion dollars of institutional capital.BitGo is a U.S.-regulated, institutional-grade digital asset infrastructure platform that supports the full digital asset lifecycle, including staking-as-a-service for secure yield, and stablecoin-as-a-service.

data.title: Bitcoin Experiences Decline Amid US Clarity Act Delays
data.description: Bitcoin has recently fallen below the $90,000 mark, influenced by delays in the US Clarity Act. According to NS3.AI, these legislative delays are considered more significant for digital assets than changes in tariff policies. Despite the volatility, Bitcoin continues to show resilience, with institutional investors prioritizing productive capital deployment over simple holding strategies. The disruption in sovereign bond markets has emphasized ongoing structural pressures, revealing vulnerabilities in traditional safe-haven assets and impacting the risk appetite within the cryptocurrency market.
